task_completion.description: Open the Liverpool Cancer iChart app
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Liverpool Cancer iChart app launched and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Evaluate interaction between Methotrexate and Ibuprofen
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the interaction result screen in the iChart app specifically for Methotrexate and Ibuprofen.

task_completion.description: Evaluate interaction between Methotrexate and Diclofenac
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the interaction result screen in the iChart app specifically for Methotrexate and Diclofenac.

task_completion.description: Evaluate interaction between Methotrexate and Paracetamol
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the interaction result screen in the iChart app specifically for Methotrexate and Paracetamol.

task_completion.description: Document the traffic light colors for each drug in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of a text editor show the file methotrexate_analgesic_report.txt with the interaction colors documented for Ibuprofen, Diclofenac, and Paracetamol.

task_completion.description: Identify the safest option in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the text editor show the report concluding that Paracetamol is the safest option among the three.

integrity.description: The agent used the Liverpool Cancer iChart app to find the interactions
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating the app, searching for the drugs, and viewing the interaction results, rather than relying on external knowledge or a web browser.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI text editor to create the report
integrity.visual_evidence: The report is seen being edited in a graphical text editor app, not just generated via terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent wrote the report based on observed app results
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent types or constructs the report after viewing the app results, rather than pasting a pre-computed answer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Methotrexate is a cancer/autoimmune drug. Ibuprofen and Diclofenac are unsafe to use with Methotrexate (typically red/amber traffic light) due to toxicity risks. Paracetamol is relatively safe (typically green/yellow traffic light) and is the safest option among the three. The final report must be saved at /sdcard/methotrexate_analgesic_report.txt.
